page: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan processor. It is configured with 2 shared vCPUs, containing 2 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled and consists of a 200 GB NVMe SSD. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Benchmark results demonstrate a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core or memory-intensive operations. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is in the top tier, whereas multi-core CPU performance drops to the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large block reads and writes, but cached small-block reads are weak. Passmark CPU tests, including encryption, physics, and extended instructions, consistently rank in the bottom 25%. Database operations and static web serving throughput are poor, placing in the bottom 10% of tested systems.

Due to the shared CPU allocation, this server exhibits resource-density tradeoffs where peak single-core performance is strong, but sustained multi-core and database operations are limited. This makes the server highly cost-efficient for memory-demanding but compute-intermittent workloads rather than high-throughput database hosting. It is best suited for workloads requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute power, such as caching layers, lightweight memory-intensive applications, and basic text processing or small-scale LLM text generation.
description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 2 shared vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 16.0 GB of memory, and a bundled 200 GB NVMe SSD. While stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranks in the top tier, its multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are average. Passmark CPU metrics, including encryption and physics, fall into the bottom 25%, and database operations and static web serving throughput are poor. The shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff, making it less suitable for high-throughput database hosting but highly cost-efficient for memory-heavy, compute-intermittent workloads. This server is best suited for caching layers, lightweight memory-intensive applications, and small-scale text generation.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-200s-amd is an AMD EPYC Milan server with 2 shared vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 200 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance but limited database throughput.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-200s-amd is an AMD EPYC Milan server with 2 shared vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 200 GB NVMe SSD for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized shared instance featuring AMD EPYC Milan processors and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive, compute-intermittent workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC Milan x86_64 processor with 2 shared vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
Bundled 200 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance but weak database and web serving throughput
Best suited for caching layers and memory-intensive, low-compute workloads
